passes.name: knowledge-curation
passes.purpose: Curate accumulated meta-knowledge from prior factory runs into a task-relevant knowledge-brief.json. On cold start (first run), produce a minimal brief acknowledging no prior signals exist. On subsequent runs, distill craft-domain signals (prose patterns, worldbuilding consistency strategies, invariant enforcement lessons) into actionable guidance for downstream passes.
passes.entryCondition: Always first — runs before any domain pass. Triggered unconditionally at pipeline start.
passes.exitCondition: knowledge-brief.json written with coldStart flag set appropriately. Single-agent pass — no coordinator needed.
passes.notes: Cold start for this run (first fantasy-writer session). Future runs will have craft-domain signals from the synthesis pass to curate. This pass is a no-op structurally but establishes the knowledge-brief.json contract for downstream consumers.

passes.name: discovery
passes.purpose: Scan the fantasy-writer domain to build a comprehensive domain model: 14 subdomains (10 sequential creative phases + 4 cross-cutting concerns), behavioral/quality/workflow/structural invariants, reusable infrastructure assets, and exemplar hierarchy patterns. Produces the domain-model.json that all subsequent passes consume.
passes.entryCondition: knowledge-brief.json exists (Pass 0 complete). No other preconditions.
passes.exitCondition: All four discovery specialists (domain-scanner, invariant-extractor, asset-auditor, exemplar-analyzer) report status=completed. domain-model.json contains subdomains, invariants, existingAssets, and exemplarPatterns arrays. Discovery coordinator confirms completeness.
passes.notes: Four specialists scan independent aspects: (1) domain-scanner extracts 14 subdomains from domain-brief.md, (2) invariant-extractor classifies 81 invariants into behavioral/quality/workflow/structural, (3) asset-auditor catalogs 26 reusable assets (7 shared-includes, 11 skills, 3 agent-templates, 4 tools, 1 MCP server), (4) exemplar-analyzer extracts 20 patterns from the fractal-factory exemplar family. All run in parallel under the coordinator. ALREADY COMPLETED in this run.

passes.name: analysis
passes.purpose: Analyze the discovered domain model to determine three architectural decisions: (1) pipeline pass structure and re-entry rules for the produced fantasy-writer system, (2) shared artifact schemas and data flow contracts between agents, (3) hierarchy depth (depth-2 vs depth-3) per coordinator based on subdomain complexity. These three analyses are independent and inform the planning pass.
passes.entryCondition: Pass 1 complete — domain-model.json exists with all four sections populated. Discovery coordinator status=completed.
passes.exitCondition: All three analysis specialists (pipeline-architect, artifact-designer, depth-analyzer) report status=completed. architecture.json contains populated pipeline, artifacts, and depth sections.
passes.notes: Three independent analyses run in parallel: pipeline-architect designs the produced system's pass structure (this agent — designing the factory pipeline); artifact-designer maps the 30+ artifact types across 14 subdomains into schemas with read-modify-write contracts; depth-analyzer evaluates each subdomain's complexity against the maxDepth=3 budget and maxAgents=80 ceiling. The high complexity of 7 subdomains (worldbuilding, character-development, plotting, chapter-drafting, revision, beta-reading, adversarial-consistency) plus 81 invariants strongly suggests depth-3 is needed for creative phases. CURRENTLY IN PROGRESS.

passes.name: planning
passes.purpose: Plan the complete agent roster (up to 80 agents), routing tables for every coordinator and the orchestrator, and golden test scenarios. Roster planning assigns agents to the 14 subdomains respecting hierarchy depth decisions. Routing planning defines the markdown routing tables with read/condition/action columns. Test planning creates scenarios covering the creative pipeline end-to-end, series production, adversarial gate failures, and craft-tool enforcement.
passes.entryCondition: Pass 2 complete — architecture.json fully populated with pipeline, artifacts, and depth sections. All three analysis agents report status=completed.
passes.exitCondition: roster.json contains all agents with assigned levels, parents, children, result codes, and artifact assignments. routing-tables.json or equivalent contains routing tables for every coordinator. test-scenarios.json contains golden test cases. Planning coordinator confirms completeness.
passes.notes: Sequential dispatch required: roster-planner runs first (routing and tests depend on knowing agent names and hierarchy). Then routing-planner builds tables using the roster. Finally test-planner creates scenarios referencing both roster and routes. The 14-subdomain, 80-agent budget with depth-3 hierarchy makes this the most complex planning pass. Key decisions: how to allocate the agent budget across 10 sequential phases + 4 cross-cutting concerns, how many specialist writers vs. reviewers per creative phase, and how adversarial auditors integrate as sub-phase gates.

passes.name: execution
passes.purpose: Write all agent prompt files (.agent.md) following the universal prompt template, and produce infrastructure files (bootstrap script, artifact schemas, skill stubs, golden test files). Uses a bounded writer→reviewer loop: prompt-writer produces prompts, prompt-reviewer adversarially checks for structural compliance, content quality, routing completeness, anti-laziness enforcement, and domain-specific correctness. Rejected prompts are re-written with reviewer feedback.
passes.entryCondition: Pass 3 complete — roster.json, routing tables, and test scenarios all exist. Planning coordinator status=completed.
passes.exitCondition: All agent prompts written and accepted by reviewer (or max retries reached with best-effort noted). Infrastructure files written. Execution coordinator confirms all roster entries have corresponding prompt files.
passes.notes: Writer→reviewer loop with maxWriterReviewerRetries=10 (from context.json). For ~80 agents, this is the longest pass. The prompt-writer must handle diverse agent types: creative specialists (worldbuilders, character designers, chapter drafters), adversarial auditors (consistency checkers, craft enforcers), structural agents (continuity trackers, series KB managers), and simulation agents (5 beta reader lenses). The reviewer must enforce: pure-router purity for coordinators, ask_questions suppression for all non-guide agents, status.json contract compliance, and domain invariant awareness. Infrastructure includes the produced system's bootstrap script, artifact directory templates, and schema files for the 30+ artifact types.

passes.name: verification
passes.purpose: Validate all produced artifacts from two independent perspectives: (1) checklist-validator runs a structural and behavioral validation checklist — file existence, template compliance, routing table completeness, artifact schema coverage, invariant enforcement traceability; (2) audit-oracle evaluates from agent-as-function and fractal-workflow-eval perspectives — contract drift, architecture violations, routing purity violations, cross-reference integrity.
passes.entryCondition: Pass 4 complete — all prompt files and infrastructure files written. Execution coordinator status=completed.
passes.exitCondition: Both verification agents report status=completed. If critical issues found, they are logged in verification findings for gap-hunting to process. No verification failure blocks progression to gap-hunting — issues are carried forward.
passes.notes: Two independent perspectives run in parallel under the coordinator. Given 81 invariants (21 behavioral, 33 quality, 13 workflow, 14 structural), the checklist-validator has substantial work tracing each invariant to its enforcement point in the produced prompts. The audit-oracle checks higher-level architecture: are all 14 subdomains covered, do routing tables form a complete DAG, do artifact contracts have matching producers and consumers, are adversarial gates actually adversarial (not rubber-stamping). The fantasy-writer domain's complexity makes this pass critical — the cross-cutting concerns (adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge, continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge) are easy to under-specify.

passes.name: gapHunting
passes.purpose: Systematically search for gaps across 9 categories using 3 specialist hunters: (1) coverage-hunter checks subdomain coverage, invariant enforcement completeness, and routing completeness; (2) artifact-hunter checks artifact schema coverage, test scenario coverage, and cross-reference integrity; (3) infrastructure-hunter checks bootstrap completeness, documentation completeness, and meta-knowledge infrastructure. Findings trigger re-entry into earlier passes for repair.
passes.entryCondition: Pass 5 complete — verification findings exist. Verification coordinator status=completed.
passes.exitCondition: All three hunters report status=completed. Gap-report.json aggregated. If gaps-found=false (zero new items across all 9 categories), convergence achieved — proceed to delivery. If gaps-found=true, trigger re-entry per re-entry rules.
passes.notes: Three specialist hunters run in parallel, each covering 3 of 9 gap categories. For the fantasy-writer domain, likely gap sources: (a) missing invariant enforcement — 81 invariants touching up to 47 agents each (SD-007 alone has 47 invariant touches) makes it likely some enforcement paths are missed; (b) craft-tool conditional invariants ('When selected: ...') need craft-profile-aware routing that may be under-specified; (c) series production paths (sequel handling, series KB updates, unresolved-threads disposition) are easy to miss on first pass; (d) 5 beta reader lenses need distinct structured-output schemas that may not all be specified. The maxGapCycles=10 allows generous iteration, but convergence should typically occur in 2-4 cycles for this domain.

passes.name: delivery
passes.purpose: Package the complete fantasy-writer agent system into the target output directory (.fractal-factory/output/romantic-fantasy-writer), write architecture documentation and user guide, and produce a final delivery report with coverage statistics, quality metrics, outstanding items, and recommendations for future iterations.
passes.entryCondition: Gap hunting converged (gaps-found=false) OR maxGapCycles reached. Gap-hunting coordinator status=completed with result indicating convergence or limit.
passes.exitCondition: All delivery agents report status=completed. Output directory contains all prompt files, infrastructure files, documentation, and the delivery report. Delivery coordinator confirms package integrity.
passes.notes: Sequential: packager assembles the output directory first (copies and organizes all agent prompts, infrastructure files, artifact schemas into the romantic-fantasy-writer prefix structure), then documentation-writer produces architecture docs and a user guide (how to invoke the produced system, expected inputs/outputs, series production workflow), then report-writer summarizes the entire factory run including coverage statistics (invariants enforced/total, subdomains covered/total, agents produced/budget), quality metrics from verification, and any outstanding gap items. This is the terminal pass.

reEntryRules.trigger: Gap-hunting coordinator finds new items requiring architectural analysis (e.g., missing artifact schemas, incorrect depth assignments, pipeline pass structure issues)
reEntryRules.notes: Heaviest re-entry — resets nearly the entire pipeline. Should be rare; typically only triggered if a fundamental architectural assumption was wrong (e.g., depth-2 is insufficient for a subdomain originally assessed as medium complexity). Deletes status.json for all agents in passes 2-6.

reEntryRules.trigger: Gap-hunting coordinator finds new items requiring roster or routing changes (e.g., missing agents for a subdomain, incomplete routing paths, missing test scenarios)
reEntryRules.notes: Moderate re-entry — adds or modifies agents in the roster and re-executes downstream. Most likely trigger: gap hunter discovers a subdomain aspect (e.g., series sequel handling, craft-tool conditional enforcement) that needs a dedicated agent not in the original roster. Routing tables are rebuilt to include new agents.

reEntryRules.trigger: Gap-hunting coordinator finds items requiring only prompt rewrites or infrastructure updates (e.g., missing invariant enforcement in existing prompts, incomplete artifact schemas, missing skill references)
reEntryRules.notes: Lightest meaningful re-entry — the most common trigger. Typical causes: invariant enforcement gaps (81 invariants across 14 subdomains = many enforcement paths to check), missing cross-references between agent prompts, incomplete craft-tool conditional logic in auditor prompts. Only resets execution, verification, and gap-hunting. High maxReEntries because prompt-level fixes are cheap.

reEntryRules.trigger: Verification finds critical structural violations that invalidate prompt correctness (e.g., routing table references non-existent agents, artifact producer/consumer mismatch)
reEntryRules.notes: Verification-triggered re-entry — bypasses gap hunting. Only triggers on critical (not major/minor) findings from the verification pass. Resets execution and verification only; gap hunting runs fresh after the fix. Prevents the need for a full gap-hunting cycle when the issue is clearly identified by verification.

convergence.convergenceSignal: Gap-hunting coordinator aggregates all three specialist hunter reports and finds gaps-found=false — zero new items across all 9 categories (subdomain coverage, invariant enforcement, routing completeness, artifact coverage, test coverage, cross-reference integrity, bootstrap completeness, documentation completeness, meta-knowledge infrastructure).
convergence.limitBehavior: Proceed to delivery (Pass 7) with all outstanding gap items flagged in the delivery report. The report-writer categorizes unresolved gaps by severity and provides remediation guidance. No gap item blocks delivery — the produced system ships with known limitations documented.
convergence.expectedCycles: 2-4 cycles expected for the fantasy-writer domain. First cycle likely catches invariant enforcement gaps (81 invariants × 14 subdomains = high surface area) and series-production edge cases. Second cycle catches cross-reference integrity issues from first-cycle prompt additions. Third cycle should be clean for most categories. Fourth cycle is insurance for the craft-tool conditional invariants (26 'When selected' invariants requiring craft-profile-aware routing).
convergenceAccelerators: Gap hunters report declining item counts per cycle — if total new items drops below 5, convergence is likely on next cycle
RE-003 (prompt-level fixes) is preferred over RE-002 (roster changes) when possible, to minimize blast radius
Verification-triggered RE-004 handles critical issues inline without consuming a gap-hunting cycle

artifacts.designNotes: Artifacts for the PRODUCED romantic-fantasy-writer agent system. These schemas define how the ~80 agents in the produced novel-writing pipeline communicate. Organized by: (1) universal infrastructure artifacts, (2) domain-specific artifacts grouped by creative phase, (3) cross-cutting tracker artifacts. All paths are relative to the per-book output directory (e.g., books/book-1/).

universal.name: story-config.json
universal.purpose: User-provided story parameters: idea, target word count, mood/tone, optional style samples, character sketches, world fragments, constraints. The single input contract for the entire pipeline.

fields.name: seriesId
fields.description: Series identifier if part of a series, null for standalone

fields.name: storyIdea
fields.description: Core story premise from the user (minimum viable input per INV-067)

fields.name: targetWordCount
fields.description: Rough target word count (e.g., 100000) per INV-026

fields.name: characterSketches
fields.description: Optional rough character ideas from user

fields.name: styleSamples
fields.description: Optional reference fiction file paths for style extraction (INV-028)

fields.name: constraints
fields.description: Optional constraints (heat level, content warnings, etc.)

fields.name: sequelOf
fields.description: storyId of predecessor book if this is a sequel

fields.name: confirmedByUser
fields.description: Must be true before pipeline launch (INV-080)

universal.name: progress.json
universal.purpose: Pipeline execution state tracking for the produced system's orchestrator. Tracks which creative phase is active, per-chapter progress, and gate statuses.

fields.name: phaseStatuses
fields.description: Map of phase-name → {status, startedAt, completedAt, gateResult}

fields.name: chapterProgress
fields.description: Per-chapter status: [{chapterNum, drafted, revised, betaRead, polished}]

fields.name: currentChapter
fields.description: Chapter currently being processed (for sequential drafting per INV-012)

universal.name: manifest.json
universal.purpose: Append-only audit trail of all agent actions. Every agent prepends an entry after completing work (newest first per PATTERN-010).

fields.name: artifacts
fields.description: List of artifact paths written or modified

universal.name: agents/*/status.json
universal.purpose: Per-agent routing signal following PATTERN-003. The orchestrator/coordinators read these to make routing decisions. Each agent writes its own status on completion.

fields.name: result
fields.description: Agent-specific result code used in routing tables

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable summary of what was done

fields.name: findings
fields.description: For auditors: {critical: N, major: N, minor: N}

domainSpecific.name: story-concept.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Crystallized story concept: refined premise, thematic pillars, genre balance, comp titles, target audience, and high-level arc shapes. The foundational document all downstream phases reference.

fields.name: thematicPillars
fields.description: 2-3 core themes [{id, theme, question, argument}] per INV-063/T25

fields.name: genreBalance
fields.description: {fantasyWeight: 0-1, romanceWeight: 0-1} — must sum to ~1, neither zero per INV-001

fields.name: compTitles
fields.description: 2-4 comparable published titles with brief rationale

fields.name: toneContract
fields.description: Tonal promise: {primary, secondary, forbiddenTones} per INV-043/T5

domainSpecific.name: craft-profile.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Story Craft Profile selecting which of the 26 craft tools (T1–T26) apply to this story. Once set, selected tools become binding invariants for the rest of production (INV-069, INV-079). Minimum 5-8 tools per INV-078.

fields.name: toolCount
fields.description: Count of selected tools (must be ≥5 per INV-078)

fields.name: selectionRationale
fields.description: Why these tools suit this specific story

fields.name: bindingFrom
fields.description: Phase at which selection was finalized (concept or plotting)

domainSpecific.designNote: AC-002 fix (gap cycle 1): Pre-depth-analysis design had concept-developer + plot-architect as dual writers. After depth analysis, plot-architect was split into structure-selector, dual-arc-builder, tension-mapper, chapter-outliner, scene-beat-designer — none write craft-profile.json. The actual sole writer is craft-profile-selector (concept phase). INV-079 plotting-phase tool additions are enforced by the plotting-auditor reading craft-profile.json, not by writing to it.

domainSpecific.name: world-bible/geography.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Geography and settings: locations, maps, climate zones, key landmarks, travel distances/methods. Independently loadable subcategory file (INV-074).

fields.name: locations
fields.description: [{id: 'LOC-NNN', name, type, description, climate, significance, connectedTo: ['LOC-NNN']}]

fields.name: travelRules
fields.description: How characters move between locations, travel times

domainSpecific.name: world-bible/magic-system.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Magic system design following Sanderson's Laws (INV-048/T10): rules, costs, limitations, known practitioners, power levels. Hard magic requires clear rules before conflict resolution.

fields.name: powerLevels
fields.description: Tiered power levels from weakest to strongest

fields.name: forbiddenUses
fields.description: What magic explicitly cannot do (prevents deus ex machina per INV-009)

domainSpecific.name: world-bible/politics.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Political and power structures: factions, governance systems, alliances, conflicts, power dynamics between groups.

fields.name: factions
fields.description: [{id: 'FAC-NNN', name, type, goals, allies: [], enemies: [], leader}]

fields.name: governanceSystems
fields.description: Types of governance across the world

domainSpecific.name: world-bible/culture.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Cultural norms: customs, religions, social hierarchies, daily life, naming conventions, festivals, taboos. Prevents anachronisms per INV-018.

fields.name: cultures
fields.description: [{id: 'CUL-NNN', name, customs, socialStructure, religion, taboos, naming}]

fields.name: socialRules
fields.description: What is/isn't acceptable behavior in this world

domainSpecific.name: world-bible/history.json
domainSpecific.purpose: World history timeline: key events, legends, era boundaries, historical figures. Establishes what 'happened before' for foreshadowing and backstory.

fields.name: legends
fields.description: Myths and legends characters might reference

domainSpecific.name: characters/index.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Character roster: all named characters with roles, relationships, and POV status. Master index cross-referencing per-character files.

domainSpecific.name: characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Per-character profile: psychological wound, desire, arc, voice parameters, sensory signature. One file per named character (INV-074). Voice parameters enable INV-003/INV-034 verification.

fields.name: isPOV
fields.description: Whether this character has POV chapters

fields.name: voiceFingerprint
fields.description: {vocabularyLevel, sentenceLength, metaphorDensity, emotionalRegister, thoughtPatterns, dialectMarkers} per INV-055/T17

fields.name: agency
fields.description: How this character drives plot through active choices (INV-008)

schema.itemSchema: One file per character, filename matches ID

domainSpecific.name: romance-arc-design.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Detailed romance arc between the leads: stages of attraction, key moments, obstacles (internal and external per INV-046/T8), the black moment (INV-045/T7), and resolution (minimum HFN per INV-001). Vulnerability escalation ladder per INV-059/T21.

domainSpecific.name: plot-structure.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Overall story structure: selected framework, act boundaries, key plot beats, subplot registry, stakes escalation (INV-044/T6). The architectural blueprint for chapter outlines.

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to story-concept.json, characters/*, romance-arc-design.json

domainSpecific.name: dual-arc-timeline.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Fantasy and romance arcs plotted in parallel (INV-050/T12). Maps where each arc's beats land per chapter, showing reinforcement and counterpoint patterns.

domainSpecific.name: chapter-outlines/{N}.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Per-chapter outline: POV character, scene goals, conflict, emotional arc, beat assignments. Must exist before drafting begins (INV-010). One file per chapter.

fields.name: pov
fields.description: CHAR-NNN of the POV character for this chapter

fields.name: povTransitionMotivation
fields.description: Why POV switches here (INV-072) — null if same POV as previous

fields.name: scenes
fields.description: [{id: 'SCN-NNN', goal, conflict, disaster, valueShift, fiveCommandments}] per INV-039/T1, INV-040/T2, INV-041/T3

fields.name: emotionalArc
fields.description: {startState, endState, pivotMoment} for the POV character

fields.name: fantasyBeats
fields.description: Plot beats from dual-arc-timeline for this chapter

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to plot-structure.json, characters/{pov}.json, dual-arc-timeline.json

schema.itemSchema: One file per chapter, filename matches chapter number

domainSpecific.name: tension-map.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Tension rise-and-fall chart across all chapters (INV-051/T13). Used during plotting to ensure pacing variation (INV-020) and during revision/beta-reading for pacing verification.

fields.name: consecutiveHighCount
fields.description: Max consecutive high-tension chapters (should not exceed 3 per INV-020)

domainSpecific.name: style-guide.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Prose style specification: voice calibration per POV character, scene-type tone palettes, prose rules (metaphor density, sentence rhythm, dialogue ratio). Based on style sample analysis if provided (INV-028, INV-024).

fields.name: perPOVCalibration
fields.description: [{charId: 'CHAR-NNN', voiceFingerprint, specificRules, exampleSentences}] — calibrated from character voice fingerprints (INV-055/T17)

fields.name: proseQualityFloor
fields.description: Measurable quality thresholds per INV-017 (max clichés, adverb limits, etc.)

fields.name: styleSampleAnalysis
fields.description: If style samples were provided: extracted abstract patterns (INV-024 — transformative only, no copying)

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to story-concept.json, characters/*, craft-profile.json

domainSpecific.name: chapters/{N}/draft.md
domainSpecific.purpose: Chapter prose draft. Written sequentially (INV-012) — chapter N+1 cannot start until N is complete. Contains the actual fiction prose with scene breaks marked.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Full chapter prose with scene break markers (----)

fields.name: frontmatter
fields.description: YAML frontmatter: chapterNum, pov, wordCount, sceneCount, draftVersion

multiWriterRules.note: AC-005 fix (gap cycle 2): roster.json declares 4 writers through creative-writing-coordinator and quality-integration-coordinator sub-pipelines. All 4 agent prompts confirm this sequential editing pattern.

domainSpecific.name: chapters/{N}/metadata.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Per-chapter structured metadata: craft tool compliance, scene value shifts, MRU adherence, emotional state tracking. Machine-readable companion to the prose draft.

fields.name: sceneBreakdown
fields.description: [{sceneId: 'SCN-NNN', goal, conflict, disaster, valueShiftFrom, valueShiftTo, sequelReaction}] per INV-039/T1, INV-040/T2

fields.name: craftToolCompliance
fields.description: Per selected tool from craft-profile: {toolId: {compliant: bool, evidence, notes}}

fields.name: foreshadowingPlants
fields.description: [{plantId, description, targetPayoffChapter}] — new plants in this chapter

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to chapter-outlines/{N}.json, style-guide.json, continuity-tracker.json

domainSpecific.name: revision-reports/{N}/developmental.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Developmental edit findings for chapter N: plot holes, pacing issues, motivation gaps, arc satisfaction, thematic drift. First of three mandatory review passes (INV-013).

fields.name: summary
fields.description: {critical: N, major: N, minor: N, overallAssessment}

domainSpecific.name: revision-reports/{N}/line-edit.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Line edit findings for chapter N: prose quality, voice distinctness, cliché detection, show-vs-tell (INV-005), repetition, dialogue naturalism (INV-019). Second mandatory pass.

fields.name: proseQualityMetrics
fields.description: {clichéCount, adverbCount, passiveVoicePercent, showTellRatio} checked against INV-017 floor

domainSpecific.name: revision-reports/{N}/copy-edit.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Copy edit findings for chapter N: grammar, naming consistency, timeline accuracy, factual accuracy within world rules (INV-018), formatting. Third mandatory pass.

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to chapters/{N}/draft.md, world-bible/*, continuity-tracker.json

domainSpecific.name: chapters/{N}/revised.md
domainSpecific.purpose: Revised chapter prose incorporating all three edit passes. Every change must cite which finding prompted it (INV-014). Replaces draft.md as the working version.

fields.name: frontmatter
fields.description: YAML frontmatter: chapterNum, pov, wordCount, revisionVersion, findingsAddressed: [ids]

domainSpecific.notes: May be overwritten if beta-reading triggers a revision cycle (INV-076). Each version increments revisionVersion.

domainSpecific.name: beta-feedback/{N}/romance-lens.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Romance reader lens: evaluates emotional satisfaction, chemistry, pacing of romantic beats, HFN/HEA delivery, heat level consistency, relationship believability.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: {critical: N, major: N, minor: N, emotionalSatisfaction: 1-10}

fields.name: arcProgressCheck
fields.description: Is romance arc on track for this chapter? {expected, actual, assessment}

domainSpecific.name: beta-feedback/{N}/fantasy-lens.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Fantasy reader lens: evaluates worldbuilding immersion, magic system consistency, plot logic, action sequences, world rules adherence.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: {critical: N, major: N, minor: N, immersionScore: 1-10}

fields.name: magicConsistency
fields.description: Cross-reference magic use against magic-system.json rules

domainSpecific.name: beta-feedback/{N}/craft-lens.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Craft reader lens: evaluates adherence to selected craft tools from craft-profile.json, scene structure, pacing, foreshadowing, craft technique execution.

domainSpecific.name: beta-feedback/{N}/sensitivity-lens.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Sensitivity reader lens: evaluates representation, potential harmful stereotypes, cultural sensitivity, power dynamics in romance, consent portrayal.

domainSpecific.name: beta-feedback/{N}/originality-lens.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Originality reader lens: flags passages, characters, plot elements, or magic systems that too closely resemble specific published works (INV-023, INV-025). Zero-tolerance plagiarism check.

fields.name: styleSampleCheck
fields.description: If style samples were used: verify transformative-only usage (INV-024)

domainSpecific.name: beta-synthesis/{N}.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Synthesized beta feedback for chapter N: aggregates all 5 lens findings, de-duplicates, prioritizes by severity. Critical and major findings must be addressed before chapter is accepted (INV-076).

fields.name: summary
fields.description: {critical: N, major: N, minor: N, totalAcrossLenses, deduplicated}

fields.name: verdict
fields.description: accepted|revision-required — revision-required if any critical or major findings (INV-076)

fields.name: revisionPriorities
fields.description: Ordered list of findings to address, critical first

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to all 5 beta-feedback/{N}/*.json files

domainSpecific.name: chapters/{N}/final.md
domainSpecific.purpose: Final polished chapter: proofread, formatted, ready for delivery. The terminal version of each chapter.

fields.name: frontmatter
fields.description: YAML frontmatter: chapterNum, pov, wordCount, finalVersion, passedAllGates: true

domainSpecific.name: chapter-summaries/{N}.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Per-chapter summary for series KB promotion: key events, character development, world changes, unresolved threads, relationship progression.

fields.name: keyEvents
fields.description: Major plot events in this chapter

fields.name: characterDevelopment
fields.description: Per-character arc movement in this chapter

fields.name: unresolvedThreads
fields.description: Threads opened or advanced but not resolved

domainSpecific.name: delivery-report.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Final delivery summary: word counts, chapter roster, quality metrics, craft tool compliance summary, invariant adherence report, outstanding items.

fields.name: totalWordCount
fields.description: Sum of all chapter word counts

fields.name: outstandingItems
fields.description: Minor findings deferred (only minor allowed per INV-076)

fields.name: artifactManifest
fields.description: Complete list of all delivered artifacts with paths

domainSpecific.name: audit-reports/{phase}/{gate-id}.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Adversarial audit report per phase gate (INV-027). Every creative phase has a gate that challenges consistency, enforces craft tools, and applies kill-your-darlings (INV-081). Blocks progression on critical findings.

fields.name: verdict
fields.description: pass|revise|block — block if any critical findings

fields.name: craftToolEnforcement
fields.description: Per selected tool: {toolId, enforced: bool, violations: []}

fields.name: upstreamRefs
fields.description: Relative paths to all artifacts reviewed in this gate

domainSpecific.name: continuity-tracker.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Running continuity document updated after every chapter draft (INV-011). Tracks character locations, timeline, knowledge state, active promises, and naming consistency.

fields.name: timeline
fields.description: [{event, chapter, inWorldTime, characters}] — chronological event log

fields.name: characterKnowledge
fields.description: Map of CHAR-NNN → [{fact, learnedInChapter}] — what each character knows (INV-056/T18 feeds this)

fields.name: activePromises
fields.description: [{id: 'PROM-NNN', type: 'chekhov'|'foreshadow'|'subplot', description, plantedChapter, status: 'open'|'resolved', resolvedChapter}] per INV-006

fields.name: namingRegistry
fields.description: Canonical spellings of all names, places, terms

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Chapter-drafter updates characterPositions, timeline, and activePromises after each chapter. Continuity-tracker performs full consistency verification and may add corrections. Both preserve all existing entries. New entries are appended; existing entries may only have their 'status' field updated (open→resolved).

domainSpecific.name: foreshadowing-ledger.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Explicit register mapping every foreshadowing plant to its payoff (INV-053/T15). Tracks plant chapter, payoff chapter, red herrings, and completeness.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: [{id: 'FS-NNN', plant: {description, chapter, lineRef}, payoff: {description, chapter, lineRef}|null, type: 'foreshadow'|'red-herring'|'callback', status: 'planted'|'paid-off'|'abandoned'}]

fields.name: completionRate
fields.description: Percentage of plants with payoffs (target: 100% excluding intentional red herrings)

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Chapter-drafter adds new plant entries after each chapter. Craft-tracker updates payoff fields when payoffs are identified in later chapters. Existing entries are never deleted — status field transitions: planted→paid-off or planted→abandoned.

domainSpecific.name: information-asymmetry-map.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Tracks what each character knows vs. what the reader knows vs. what other characters know (INV-056/T18). Critical for maintaining dramatic irony and avoiding info leaks.
domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Chapter-drafter registers new facts and character knowledge gains. Continuity-tracker verifies no character uses information they haven't learned. Entries are append-only for facts; knownBy arrays grow as characters learn things.

domainSpecific.name: mystery-box-inventory.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Tracks active unresolved questions the reader holds (INV-061/T23). Maintains target range (typically 3-7 active boxes). Used for pacing and engagement control.

fields.name: boxes
fields.description: [{id: 'MB-NNN', question, openedChapter, closedChapter: null|N, importance: 'major'|'minor', status: 'open'|'closed'}]

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Chapter-drafter opens new boxes and updates perChapterSnapshot. Craft-tracker closes boxes when answers are delivered and recomputes activeCount. Status transitions: open→closed only.

domainSpecific.name: emotional-throughline.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Charts each lead's specific emotional state per chapter boundary (INV-058/T20). Enforces emotional variety (INV-037) — no character in same emotional state for 3+ consecutive chapters.

fields.name: characters
fields.description: Map of CHAR-NNN → [{chapter, emotionalState: granular label, dominantEmotion, intensity: 1-10, trigger}]

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Chapter-drafter appends new chapter entries to each character's array. Craft-tracker recomputes varietyCheck after each update. Character arrays are append-only.

domainSpecific.name: symbolic-motif-registry.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Tracks 3-5 recurring symbols/motifs assigned to thematic pillars (INV-054/T16). Records appearances per chapter with imagery callbacks and evolution.

fields.name: motifs
fields.description: [{id: 'MOT-NNN', symbol, linkedTheme, appearances: [{chapter, context, evolution}], targetDensity}]

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Single primary writer (craft-tracker). Initial motifs set during plotting; appearances appended per chapter.

domainSpecific.name: series-kb/index.json
domainSpecific.purpose: Series knowledge base master index. Lives at series level (not per-book). References all per-book KBs and tracks cross-book facts. Append-mostly per INV-070.

fields.name: namingConventions
fields.description: Canonical names and spellings across the series

domainSpecific.multiWriterRules: Single primary writer (series-kb-manager). Append-mostly: facts established in earlier books cannot be silently retconned (INV-070). New books append to books array and add crossBookFacts. unresolvedThreads dispositions updated during sequel concept phase.
domainSpecific.pathNote: BC-W01 fix: Artifact key is series-kb/index.json (per-book relative reference). Bootstrap creates the actual file at {root}/series/{series-id}/index.json. Agents reference it as series-kb/index.json relative to the book output directory, which symlinks or resolves to the series-level path.

dataFlow.purpose: User inputs feed concept development — story idea, constraints, style samples
Crystallized concept defines world parameters — genre, setting type, magic needs
Selected craft tools become binding constraints for plot structure design
World rules constrain character abilities, cultural backgrounds, and social dynamics
Character arcs, wounds, and relationships shape plot structure and beat placement
Romance arc stages and vulnerability ladder inform dual-arc interleave
Per-chapter outlines are mandatory input for drafting (INV-010 Outline Before Draft)
Act structure, beats, and subplot registry guide chapter-level execution
Fantasy/romance beat assignments per chapter guide arc interleaving
Tension targets per chapter guide pacing in prose
Voice calibration and prose rules constrain how chapters are written
Chapter draft is the input for three-pass editing
Structured metadata enables craft-aware developmental review
Three edit reports feed the chapter reviser (dev→line→copy→revise)
Revised chapter is the input for all five beta reader lenses
Five lens reports feed the beta synthesizer
Synthesized beta feedback may trigger revision cycle (INV-076 severity-gated)
Accepted revised chapter feeds final polish
Polished chapters assemble into the delivery package
Chapter summaries feed series KB promotion for sequel support
Updated after each chapter (INV-011); read before next chapter to maintain consistency
Tracks plant/payoff status across chapters; read to ensure planned payoffs
Prevents character info leaks — checked before writing character actions
Maintains 3-7 active questions for reader engagement pacing
Concept is the ground truth for all adversarial consistency checks
Adversarial gates produce findings that may block phase progression
For sequels: series KB provides established facts and unresolved threads (INV-071)
dataFlow.pathNote: See series-kb/index.json artifact definition for path mapping note (BC-W01).

decisions.rationale: 3 specialists (concept-developer, craft-profile-selector, concept-auditor) is well under the 8-specialist threshold. 3 cross-cutting concerns (craft-toolbox-selection, adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge-management) are at the threshold but not over. Single conceptual subdomain group. No criteria trigger depth-3.

5 distinct world-bible sub-areas (geography, magic-system, politics, culture, history) exceed the 3-group threshold. While 6 specialists are under the 8-specialist cap, the sub-areas produce structurally different artifacts with different dependency chains: physical-world areas (geography, culture, history) reference each other for spatial/temporal consistency, while systems-world areas (magic, politics) define rule systems that constrain all others. Depth-3 separates these two routing patterns.

subCoordinators.name: physical-world-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes geography, culture, and history specialists that build the tangible, experiential world. These three share heavy cross-references (geography constrains culture, history explains both).

subCoordinators.name: systems-world-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes magic-system and political-structure specialists that define the world's rule systems. Magic and politics co-constrain (who has power, how magic affects governance).

decisions.rationale: 4 cross-cutting concerns (adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge-management, multi-pov-craft, emotional-resonance) exceed the 3-concern threshold. Character development requires two distinct routing patterns: core character creation (protagonist psychology, romance arc dynamics between leads) operates on deep individual characterization, while ensemble management (supporting cast, voice differentiation across POVs) operates on consistency and distinctiveness across the full cast. The 4 cross-cutting concerns (especially multi-POV craft and emotional resonance) add routing complexity that benefits from separation.

subCoordinators.name: core-characters-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes specialists building the central character pair — protagonist depth (wounds, desires, growth arc) and the romance arc dynamic (chemistry, conflict sources, emotional escalation beats).

subCoordinators.name: ensemble-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes specialists managing supporting cast with mini-arcs, antagonist design, and per-character voice calibration ensuring POV distinctiveness.

decisions.rationale: Two criteria trigger: 4 cross-cutting concerns (adversarial-consistency, craft-knowledge-systems, continuity-tracking, reader-experience-design) AND 4 subdomain groups (structural framework selection, dual-arc timeline design, per-chapter outlining, scene/beat design). With 41 invariants (second-highest of any subdomain — 15 behavioral, 14 quality), this is the most invariant-dense planning phase. The structural-design vs chapter-design split maps naturally to sub-coordinators: structural design selects the framework, builds the macro arc timeline, and maps tension — these are whole-story decisions. Chapter-level design produces per-chapter outlines and scene beats — these are granular, iterative decisions. Different routing complexity at each level.

subCoordinators.name: structural-design-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes whole-story structural decisions: framework selection (three-act, Save the Cat, Hero's Journey), dual-arc timeline construction (fantasy plot + romance arc interleaved), and tension-map design (rise/fall across chapters).

subCoordinators.name: chapter-design-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes per-chapter granular planning: chapter outline production (POV/goal/conflict/beats per chapter) and scene-beat design (Five Commandments, emotional beats, hook placement).

decisions.rationale: 3 specialists (style-analyzer, style-guide-writer, style-auditor) is well under the 8-specialist threshold. 3 cross-cutting concerns (adversarial-consistency, multi-pov-craft, series-knowledge-management) are at the threshold but not over. Only 2 subdomain groups (analysis vs guide production). No criteria trigger depth-3.

STRONGEST depth-3 case. 4 cross-cutting concerns (adversarial-consistency, continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge-systems, multi-pov-craft) exceed the 3-concern threshold. With 47 invariants — the highest of ANY subdomain, including 26 quality invariants — this phase has the densest enforcement requirements. The per-chapter iteration pattern means the coordinator must manage two fundamentally different streams per chapter cycle: (1) the creative-writing stream (drafting prose, maintaining POV voice), and (2) the quality-integration stream (checking continuity, enforcing craft rules, running the adversarial gate). Separating these into sub-coordinators keeps routing tables manageable and makes the per-chapter loop clearer.

subCoordinators.name: creative-writing-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes the creative prose production for each chapter: the chapter-drafter writes the actual prose following the outline, and the POV-voice-maintainer ensures character voice consistency and distinctiveness per POV section.

subCoordinators.name: quality-integration-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes the quality enforcement stream per chapter: continuity-integrator checks against the continuity tracker, craft-enforcer validates selected craft-tool invariants (foreshadowing callbacks, scene commandments, emotional beats).

decisions.rationale: 4 specialists (developmental-editor, line-editor, copy-editor, revision-auditor) under the 8-specialist threshold. Only 2 cross-cutting concerns (continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge-systems). 3 subdomain groups (developmental/line/copy) are at the threshold but not over. Despite 28 invariants (third-highest), the three editing passes follow a clean sequential pattern that a single coordinator handles well. No criteria trigger depth-3.

5 distinct reader-lens subdomain groups (romance-reader, fantasy-reader, craft-reader, sensitivity-reader, originality-reader) plus a synthesis stage exceed the 3-group threshold. The 5 lenses naturally split into two clusters: genre-lens readers (romance + fantasy — assess genre promise fulfillment and world/magic satisfaction) and craft-lens readers (craft + sensitivity + originality — assess writing quality, representation, and freshness). Each cluster evaluates from a fundamentally different perspective and produces differently-structured feedback. The synthesis stage aggregates both clusters. Depth-3 cleanly maps this two-cluster fan-out pattern.

subCoordinators.name: genre-lens-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes genre-focused beta readers: romance-reader (HEA/HFN satisfaction, chemistry, emotional escalation, steam level) and fantasy-reader (worldbuilding immersion, magic consistency, plot tension, lore engagement).

subCoordinators.name: craft-lens-coordinator
subCoordinators.purpose: Routes craft-and-quality-focused beta readers: craft-reader (prose quality, pacing, structure), sensitivity-reader (representation, harmful tropes, cultural accuracy), originality-reader (cliché detection, fresh elements, comp-title differentiation).

decisions.rationale: 3 specialists (proofreader, summary-generator, series-KB-promoter) is well under all thresholds. Only 1 cross-cutting concern (series-knowledge-management). 2 subdomain groups (text finalization, packaging). Lightest coordinator in the system. No criteria trigger depth-3.

crossCuttingAgents.note: Cross-cutting subdomains (adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge-management, continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge-systems) are NOT separate coordinators. They are implemented as: (1) adversarial auditors embedded per-phase (counted as 'auditor' specialist in each phase coordinator), (2) shared specialist agents invoked by multiple coordinators, and (3) craft knowledge skills.

sharedSpecialists.name: craft-tracker
sharedSpecialists.note: Originally planned as foreshadowing-architect; merged into craft-tracker during roster planning

depth.budgetDetail: 68 of 80 agents used (85%). 12 agents of headroom available for gap-hunting additions (e.g., additional specialist for a subdomain discovered to be under-covered, or an extra auditor for a phase that needs tighter enforcement). No depth-3 decisions were reduced.
